summary.rationale: The songs UI now reads more naturally because metadata labels no longer shout in all caps while the overall hierarchy remains intact.

notes: The list-detail shell and explicit empty state remain unchanged.
Labels remain scannable without relying on all-caps styling.
The score and library surfaces now feel calmer and easier to read.
Removing all-caps labels better fits the restrained editorial direction.
Hierarchy is now carried more by spacing, weight, and contrast than by aggressive uppercase treatment.

next_steps: If more typographic tuning is needed, introduce shared metadata-label classes so spacing and tracking can be adjusted in one place.
